note: Provider submodule of fontyourface; self-hosted fonts are created via a config-entity form (file uploads), so cases are recipe (response-graded).

evals.prompt: In Drupal's @font-your-face, which submodule lets you self-host your own font files and how are fonts added?
evals.expected_output: local_fonts (provider name Custom Fonts). You add fonts by creating local_font_config_entity 'Custom Font' entities at /admin/appearance/font/local_font_config_entity, uploading woff/woff2/svg files — no remote import.

evals.prompt: What does the local_fonts submodule generate when a Custom Font is saved, and where?
evals.expected_output: On presave it writes the uploaded font files and a generated font.css to public://fontyourface/local_fonts/{id}, and upserts a matching font entity with url local_fonts://{id}.

evals.prompt: What is the fontyourface provider id for locally hosted fonts?
